title: Unified Binod Calendar (UBC): A Peer-Reviewed Civil Timekeeping Standard for the Binod World Government
authors: Chief Scientific Committee on Temporal Standards (CSCTS)
institution: Republic of Binod — Department of Chronometry and Astronomical Science
abstract: This document constitutes the official specification of the Unified Binod Calendar (UBC), the civil timekeeping standard of the Republic of Binod, ratified by the Chief Scientific Committee on Temporal Standards (CSCTS) following five years of peer-reviewed research (UBC 0-5). The calendar is anchored to the Founding Epoch of 23 April 2021 CE, the verified date of the Binodian world's first sunrise as reconstructed through archaeological and astronomical evidence. The UBC is computed from the Temporal Synchronization Index (TSI), a dimensionless coupling constant derived from the ratio of Binodian mean solar day to the SI-anchored terrestrial second. The specification defines epoch, year structure, month nomenclature, week structure, date notation, leap corrections, and bidirectional conversion formulae.
citation: Chief Scientific Committee on Temporal Standards (CSCTS). (Published 04-KYT-5, rev. 01-SEL-6). Unified Binod Calendar (UBC): A Peer-Reviewed Civil Timekeeping Standard for the Binod World Government. Binod Journal of Astronomical & Civil Science, 1(1). Document ID: UBC-STD-2026-001.
